topic: Revelation, Recording and Memorization of the Glorious Qur'an

subUnits.title: 1.1 Revelation of the Glorious Qur'an
notes: • Wahy (وحي) means divine inspiration or revelation from Allah to His Prophets
• The Qur'an was revealed to Prophet Muhammad (SAW) through Angel Jibreel (Gabriel)
• First revelation came in the Cave of Hira during the month of Ramadan
• Occurred in 610 CE when Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was 40 years old
• First verses revealed were the first five verses of Surah Al-Alaq
1. Read in the name of your Lord who created
2. Created man from a clinging substance
3. Read, and your Lord is the Most Generous
5. Taught man that which he knew not
Surah Al-Qadr (97:1) confirms the night of revelation:
Indeed, We sent the Qur'an down during the Night of Decree
• The Qur'an was revealed over a period of 23 years
1. Through Angel Jibreel in human form
2. Through inspiration directly to the heart
4. Sometimes with sound like the ringing of a bell
And indeed, it is the revelation of the Lord of the worlds. The Trustworthy Spirit (Jibreel) has brought it down. Upon your heart that you may be of the warners
• Emphasized the importance of knowledge and reading
• Established the foundation of Islamic education
• Showed Allah's mercy in guiding humanity

subUnits.youtubeQuery: First revelation Quran Cave of Hira SSS1 IRS site:youtube.com

quiz.q: The first revelation came to Prophet Muhammad (SAW) in:
quiz.explanation: The first revelation came in the Cave of Hira on the Mountain of Light (Jabal an-Nur) near Makkah.

quiz.q: The first verses revealed were from Surah:
quiz.explanation: The first five verses of Surah Al-Alaq (96:1-5) were the first revelation.

quiz.q: The Angel who brought revelation was:
quiz.explanation: Angel Jibreel (Gabriel) was entrusted with bringing revelation from Allah to the Prophets.

quiz.q: The Qur'an was revealed over a period of:
quiz.explanation: The Qur'an was revealed over 23 years: 13 years in Makkah and 10 years in Madinah.

quiz.q: The first word revealed was:
quiz.explanation: The first word revealed was 'Iqra' (Read) as in Surah Al-Alaq 96:1.

quiz.q: The night when the Qur'an was first revealed is called:
quiz.explanation: Laylatul Qadr (Night of Decree) is when the Qur'an was first revealed, as mentioned in Surah Al-Qadr.

quiz.q: How many verses were revealed in the first revelation?
quiz.explanation: The first five verses of Surah Al-Alaq (96:1-5) were revealed in the first revelation.

quiz.q: Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was how old when he received the first revelation?
quiz.explanation: Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was 40 years old when he received the first revelation in 610 CE.

quiz.q: The Qur'an emphasizes in the first revelation the importance of:
quiz.explanation: The first revelation emphasized reading and knowledge, showing the importance of education in Islam.

quiz.q: Surah Al-Qadr mentions that the Qur'an was revealed in:
quiz.explanation: Surah Al-Qadr (97:1) states the Qur'an was revealed in the Night of Decree, which is in Ramadan.

subUnits.title: 1.2 Recording and Memorization of the Qur'an
notes: Recording of the Qur'an During the Prophet's Time:
• The Qur'an was recorded on various materials available at that time
3. Shoulder blades of camels and sheep (Al-Aktaf)
• The primary method of preserving the Qur'an was through memorization
• Prophet Muhammad (SAW) himself memorized every verse
• Many Companions (Sahabah) memorized the entire Qur'an
1. Revelation: Angel Jibreel would bring verses
3. Recitation: Prophet would recite to Companions
4. Writing: Scribes would write on available materials
5. Verification: Jibreel would review annually with Prophet
لَا تُحَرِّكْ بِهِ لِسَانَكَ لِتَعْجَلَ بِهِ
Move not your tongue with it to hasten it. Indeed, upon Us is its collection and recitation. So when We have recited it, then follow its recitation. Then upon Us is its clarification
• Angel Jibreel would review the entire Qur'an with the Prophet once every year
• In the year of the Prophet's death, Jibreel reviewed it twice
Notable scribes who wrote down revelation:
• This dual method ensured perfect preservation
• Even if written records were lost, memorizers preserved it
• Even if memorizers died, written records preserved it
• Made the Qur'an accessible to all, literate or illiterate
• Established the tradition of Hifz that continues today

quiz.q: The Qur'an was recorded on all the following EXCEPT:
quiz.explanation: The Qur'an was recorded on simple, available materials like palm leaves, stones, leather, and bones, not on expensive gold tablets.

quiz.q: The primary method of preserving the Qur'an was through:
quiz.explanation: While writing was used, memorization (Hifz) was the primary and most reliable method of preservation.

quiz.q: Who was the chief scribe of revelation?
quiz.explanation: Zaid ibn Thabit was the chief scribe who wrote down most of the revelation during the Prophet's time.

quiz.q: How often did Jibreel review the Qur'an with the Prophet?
quiz.explanation: Jibreel reviewed the entire Qur'an with the Prophet once every year, and twice in the year of his death.

quiz.q: Which Companion is NOT listed among the famous memorizers?
quiz.explanation: Abu Jahl was an enemy of Islam, not a Companion or memorizer of the Qur'an.

quiz.q: The dual preservation method refers to:
quiz.explanation: The dual method was writing on materials and memorization by heart, providing double security for preservation.

quiz.q: Shoulder blades used for recording were from:
quiz.explanation: The shoulder blades (Al-Aktaf) of camels and sheep were used as writing materials.

quiz.q: The verse about not moving the tongue hastily is in Surah:
quiz.explanation: Surah Al-Qiyamah (75:16-19) addresses the Prophet about not rushing to memorize the revelation.

quiz.q: In the year of the Prophet's death, Jibreel reviewed the Qur'an:
quiz.explanation: In the final year, Jibreel reviewed the Qur'an twice with the Prophet, indicating the completion of revelation.

quiz.q: The system of preservation ensured:
quiz.explanation: The dual system of writing and memorization guaranteed the perfect authenticity and preservation of the Qur'an.
